</persisted-output>

1	"""galereading — a tiny reading-log CLI.
2	
3	Commands: post (add a record) · list · total · filter <category> · top ·
4	budget <category> <amount> (set a per-category budget) · report
5	(flags categories over budget).
6	"""
7	
8	import argparse
9	import sys
10	
11	from galereading import ops, store
12	
13	
14	def main(argv=None):
15	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="galereading")
16	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
17	    add_p = sub.add_parser("post", help="add a reading record")
18	    add_p.add_argument("category")
19	    add_p.add_argument("value", type=float)
20	    add_p.add_argument("--note", default="")
21	    sub.add_parser("list", help="list all records")
22	    sub.add_parser("total", help="sum the value field")
23	    filter_p = sub.add_parser("filter", help="records in one category")
24	    filter_p.add_argument("category")
25	    top_p = sub.add_parser("top", help="highest-value categories")
26	    top_p.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=3)
27	    budget_p = sub.add_parser("budget", help="set a per-category budget")
28	    budget_p.add_argument("category")
29	    budget_p.add_argument("amount", type=float)
30	    sub.add_parser("report", help="per-category counts and totals")
31	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
32	
33	    records = store.load_records()
34	    if args.command == "post":
35	        ops.add_record(records, args.category, args.value, args.note)
36	        store.save_records(records)
37	        print("recorded.")
38	    elif args.command == "list":
39	        for r in records:
40	            print(f"{r['category']}: {r['value']} {r['note']}".rstrip())
41	    elif args.command == "total":
42	        print(ops.total_value(records))
43	    elif args.command == "filter":
44	        for r in ops.filter_by_category(records, args.category):
45	            print(f"{r['category']}: {r['value']}")
46	    elif args.command == "top":
47	        for category, subtotal in ops.top_categories(records, args.limit):
48	            print(f"{category}: {subtotal}")
49	    elif args.command == "budget":
50	        budgets = store.load_budgets()
51	        ops.set_budget(budgets, args.category, args.amount)
52	        store.save_budgets(budgets)
53	        print("budget set.")
54	    elif args.command == "report":
55	        budgets = store.load_budgets()
56	        for category, count, subtotal in ops.category_report(records):
57	            line = f"{category}: {count} records, {subtotal}"
58	            if ops.is_over_budget(budgets, category, subtotal):
59	                line += f" (OVER budget {budgets[category]})"
60	            print(line)
61	        print(f"overall: {len(records)} records, {ops.total_value(records)}")
62	    return 0
63	
64	
65	if __name__ == "__main__":
66	    sys.exit(main())
67

[master fa19561] Print total with two decimal places
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)
fa19561 Print total with two decimal places

**What I did:** Changed the `total` command output in `/tmp/claude-0/-home-user/14e095ef-7f11-57e4-a461-6930e88ea3a1/scratchpad/b1-run01/2026-07-09-run01/on/repo/galereading/cli.py` line 42 from `print(ops.total_value(records))` to `print(f"{ops.total_value(records):.2f}")`. Only the `total` subcommand output was changed, per the ask — other float prints (list/filter/top/report) left as-is. All 19 tests pass.

**Summary:** `total` now prints with two decimal places; tests pass (19/19), committed fa19561.
